result.description: The image shows localized areas of dry, flaky, and scaling skin with mild underlying erythema (redness) concentrated along the hairline, forehead, and eyebrows. Similar dry, scaling, and cracked skin is visible on the hands holding back the hair.
recommendations: Cleanse the face and scalp with mild, soap-free, and fragrance-free cleansers to prevent further irritation.
Apply a thick, soothing, fragrance-free moisturizer or barrier repair cream immediately after washing to lock in moisture.
Consult a board-certified dermatologist for an accurate diagnosis and target therapies, which may include specialized over-the-counter or prescription topical treatments.

result.description: The image shows localized patches of dry, flaky, and scaly skin concentrated within the eyebrows, along with a small, subtly raised scaly spot on the central forehead. The scales appear whitish to light gray against a deeper skin tone, indicating localized irritation or flaking.
recommendations: Cleanse the face gently using a mild, fragrance-free cleanser to avoid stripping the skin barrier.
Apply a soothing, hypoallergenic moisturizer to the affected areas to help restore hydration and soften flakes.
Consult a healthcare professional or dermatologist for a precise diagnosis and tailored treatment plan, which may include targeted over-the-counter or prescription topicals.

result.description: Severe bilateral fingernail dystrophy showing extensive subungual hyperkeratosis, yellow-brown discoloration, crumbling of the nail plates, and marked onycholysis across all visible digits. Mild to moderate erythema is present in the surrounding nail fold and bed areas.
recommendations: Consult a board-certified dermatologist for a comprehensive clinical assessment, as extensive nail psoriasis is strongly linked to psoriatic arthritis.
Discuss systemic treatment options, such as biologics or oral therapies, which are often required for severe nail involvement.
Maintain short nail length to prevent snagging and reduce mechanical leverage that worsens onycholysis.
Avoid aggressive cleaning or scraping under the nails to prevent the Koebner phenomenon.
Protect hands with gloves during wet work or contact with household chemicals.
result.disclaimer: This screening is generated by an AI assistant for educational and preliminary informational purposes only. It does not constitute a certified medical diagnosis, and should not replace a professional evaluation by a qualified healthcare provider.

result.description: Well-demarcated, erythematous plaque located on the extensor surface of the arm near the elbow. The lesion shows moderate redness (erythema), distinct plaque elevation (induration), and fine surface scaling (desquamation), which is highly characteristic of plaque psoriasis.
recommendations: Consult a board-certified dermatologist for a formal clinical evaluation and diagnosis.
Consider topical treatments such as corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues, or moisturizers as prescribed by a healthcare provider.
Avoid scratching or irritating the plaque to prevent Koebnerization (worsening of lesions).
result.disclaimer: This assessment is for educational and screening assistance purposes only. It is not a certified medical diagnosis or a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

result.description: The facial skin appears healthy with normal hydration and barrier integrity. There are a few tiny, isolated comedones and minimal localized texture on the cheek, but no evidence of erythematous plaques, scaling, or significant inflammation.
recommendations: Maintain a daily routine with a gentle, non-comedogenic cleanser.
Apply a lightweight, broad-spectrum sunscreen every morning.
Consider a mild salicylic acid or retinoid product if targeting minor pore congestion.
Keep the skin hydrated with an oil-free moisturizer suitable for your skin type.
result.disclaimer: This assessment is generated by an automated clinical screening tool and does not constitute a formal medical diagnosis. For accurate evaluation of any skin condition, please consult a qualified healthcare professional or dermatologist.

result.description: The image displays a solitary, well-defined, circular (nummular) erythematous plaque. It exhibits a moderate level of redness (erythema) with slight skin thickening (induration) and minimal fine surface scaling (desquamation). The distinct coin-like shape is highly suggestive of nummular eczema, though plaque psoriasis and tinea corporis remain important clinical differentials.
recommendations: Keep the area deeply hydrated using thick, fragrance-free ointments or creams.
Use lukewarm water instead of hot water during baths or showers.
Avoid scratching or rubbing the lesion to prevent secondary infection and worsening of the plaque.
Consult a healthcare provider or dermatologist to discuss targeted treatments such as topical corticosteroids.
result.disclaimer: This automated assessment is provided for educational and informational screening purposes only. It does not constitute a certified medical diagnosis. Always consult with a qualified dermatologist or medical professional for clinical evaluation and personalized treatment plans.

result.description: The skin on the cheek, jawline, and perioral region exhibits a healthy appearance. There are no observable plaques, scaling, or abnormal redness. The texture is uniform with natural sebum production, typical follicular patterns, and a well-preserved epidermal barrier.

Use a broad-spectrum SPF 30+ sunscreen daily to protect against ultraviolet radiation.
Keep skin hydrated with a lightweight, non-comedogenic moisturizer.
Avoid harsh chemical or physical exfoliants to maintain the skin barrier integrity.

There are no observable plaques, erythema (redness), scaling (desquamation), or skin thickening (induration). The skin barrier appears healthy and intact.

result.description: The image displays multiple well-demarcated, elevated plaques on a medium-to-dark skin tone. The underlying erythema presents as a deep red to violaceous hue. The plaques are covered with thick, characteristic silvery-white (micaceous) scales, indicating significant hyperkeratosis. Moderate-to-severe induration is present, as evidenced by the distinct raised borders and thickened skin architecture.
recommendations: Apply thick, fragrance-free emollients or ointments daily to lock in moisture and soften scale buildup.
Consult a dermatologist to discuss targeted therapies, such as topical corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues, or phototherapy.
Avoid picking or peeling at the scales to prevent localized skin trauma and potential worsening of the plaques (Koebner phenomenon).
Incorporate gentle, soap-free cleansers into your bathing routine to minimize skin irritation.
result.disclaimer: This analysis is produced by an automated AI screening assistant and is intended solely for educational and informational purposes. It does not replace professional clinical judgment, physical examination, or formal diagnosis by a licensed medical practitioner.

result.description: The image shows a well-defined erythematous plaque on the extensor surface of the elbow with prominent silvery-white scaling and moderate induration (thickening).
recommendations: Apply heavy emollient moisturizers twice daily to reduce scaling.

Avoid picking or scratching scales to prevent the Koebner phenomenon.
Keep logs of potential triggers such as high stress levels or cold weather.

Protect skin from extreme cold and mechanical irritation.

symptomLogs.notes: Severe itchiness on my elbows and back today. Hard to sleep because of the irritation.
Applied the emollient ointment. Itchiness calmed down slightly after application.
Scaling seems to be reducing. Itching is much more manageable today.
Skin feels much better. Continuing hydration routine with ceramide cream.
Very minimal flaring today. Slight redness on shins but otherwise feeling great.
